sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven are struggling, having lost their last three matches and conceding multiple goals in each. SV Zulte Waregem are also in poor form, winless in their last five games, with draws and losses dominating their recent results. Both teams have shown defensive vulnerabilities, often conceding in recent outings.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, results have been split with SV Zulte Waregem winning 2-0 at home in September 2025, while Oud-Heverlee Leuven dominated with a 5-0 victory at home in December 2024. Historically, matches between these sides often see both teams scoring, with 8 of their last 10 meetings producing goals for both.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leuven, sitting 14th with 15 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 26 goals (13th in the league), while their attack has managed only 17 goals. Zulte Waregem, 9th with 21 points, have a more balanced record with 22 goals scored and conceded, showing slightly better form and efficiency in front of goal.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Leuven, Youssef Maziz (2 goals, 2 assists) and Mathieu Maertens are pivotal in attack, while Zulte Waregem rely heavily on Jeppe Erenbjerg, the league’s top scorer with 7 goals. Erenbjerg’s form could be decisive in exploiting Leuven’s leaky defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Despite playing at home, Leuven’s poor form and defensive issues make them underdogs against a Zulte Waregem side with a stronger attacking threat. Sofascore’s prediction favors an away win (46% probability), which aligns with current trends. Expect a competitive match with goals likely on both sides given recent patterns.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the score between Oud-Heverlee Leuven and SV Zulte Waregem stands at 1-1, with both teams showing moments of promise. Thibaud Verlinden opened the scoring for Leuven early in the game, while Benoit Nyssen equalized for Waregem later in the half. Both goals came from well-executed right-footed strikes, highlighting individual quality in the attacking third. The game remains finely poised, with neither side establishing clear dominance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team statistics comparison
halftimeSections.text: SV Zulte Waregem have controlled possession for the majority of the half, holding the ball significantly more than Oud-Heverlee Leuven. Waregem also recorded more entries into the final third and penalty area, suggesting a more aggressive approach in attack. However, Leuven have been more clinical with their chances, managing more shots on target despite fewer attacking entries. Defensively, Leuven have shown strength in aerial duels, while Waregem have edged out in overall duels won. This balance indicates a tight contest heading into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players analysis
halftimeSections.text: For Oud-Heverlee Leuven, Thibaud Verlinden has been the standout performer, not only scoring but also contributing to the team’s attacking threat. Sory Kaba has also shown promise in the forward line with a high expected goals contribution and involvement in key plays. On the defensive end, Noë Dussenne has been solid with multiple clearances. For SV Zulte Waregem, Benoit Nyssen’s goal has been crucial, and his impact in the final third stands out. Tochukwu Nnadi’s assist highlights his creative influence in midfield. Both teams have players capable of turning the game in their favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. SV Zulte Waregem started strongly, dictating the early stages with sustained attacking pressure. However, Oud-Heverlee Leuven responded with a significant shift in momentum, capitalizing on a key spell to take the lead. Waregem fought back with several periods of dominance in the middle and latter parts of the half, eventually finding their equalizer through persistent attacking intent. Towards the end, momentum seemed to tilt slightly in Leuven’s favor with a late push, setting up an intriguing second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Given the balance of play in the first half, the second half is likely to remain competitive. Oud-Heverlee Leuven will look to build on their late momentum and clinical finishing to create more chances. Sory Kaba could be a key figure if Leuven capitalize on their attacking opportunities. SV Zulte Waregem, with their superior possession and territorial advantage, might push for control and exploit their entries into dangerous areas. Defensive solidity will be crucial for both teams, as any lapse could prove costly in such a tight encounter. Expect both sides to come out with intent, potentially leading to an open and entertaining half.
